qas.q: Where are munitions safety requirements found?
qas.a:
AFMAN 91-201, Explosive Safety Standards

TO 11a-1-33 Handling and Maintenance of Explosives Loaded Aircraft

qas.q: What is the cardinal rule for explosive safety?
qas.a: Minimum number of people, minimum amount of explosives, minimum amount of time

qas.q: Personnel working with explosives must be trained in accordance with what?
qas.a: AFI 91-202, Mishap Prevention

qas.q: What must supervisors do?
qas.a: Knowledgeable of all hazards. Knows/conveys emergency procedures, strict housekeeping

qas.q: What is important in regards to compatibility groups?
qas.a: Ideally each group is separated, compatible explosives may be combined. Not applicable between MSA and Flight Line

qas.q: What is a licensed location?
qas.a: Small amounts of explosives. Outside MSA (egress, life support, SF). Approved by wing safety (licensed facilities not necessarily on the explosive site plan, lice displayed at facility, not based on quantity distance Commander approves local procedures. No H/D 1.1 allowed. No compatibility groups A, K, and L allowed. NOT BASED on Quantity Distance

qas.q: What are the maximum licensed quantities for a licensed location?
qas.a:
H/D 1.1 - zero

H/D 1.2.2 - 100 pounds

H/D 1.3 - 100 pounds

H/D 1.4 - Mission essential quantities.

Local restrictions may be more limiting, turn in un-serviceable explosives ASAP

qas.q: What is a sited location?
qas.a: Very large amounts. Inside and outside MSA. Approved by DDESB. Explosives site planning is a process used to manage the risks associated with explosives activities to ensure the minimum risk to personnel, equipment, and assets, while meeting mission requirements. Area containing large amounts of explosives (identified in the ESP). Explosive site plan is submitted by wing safety. Based on quantity distance (QD). States max quantities of H/Ds stored

qas.q: What must installation maps show?
qas.a: Explosive clear zones. Affects where real property can be built. Violations of QD require waiver at the SECAF Level and interim solutions.

qas.q: What must always be posted at all explosive locations?
qas.a: Explosive limits - posted signs show authorized H/D and net explosive weight quantity distance (NEWQD) limits. Personnel Limits (supervisors, workers, casuals, visitors). Casual personnel are not normally part of explosive operation. Have duties which require their presence. Visitors are non-essential personnel with limited access. Stop operations when visitors are present.

qas.q: What are limits on static/public display aircraft?
qas.a: No live ammo, aircraft gun systems empty, safed. Constant surveillance of visitors. Cannot touch or sit in external release systems, egress seats. Exempted items.

qas.q: What does TO 11A-1-33 cover?
qas.a: Handling and maintenance of explosives loaded aircraft

qas.q: When are aircraft considered non-explosive loaded aircraft?
qas.a:
Egress components

Authorized signals/flares

Destructors installed in
sensitive electronics

Fire extinguisher cartridges

Engine starter cartridges

Small arms ammo for survival use

Inert training munitions

qas.q: What happens with/to explosive-loaded aircraft?
qas.a: They will be separated and will comply with QD. Parked in designated areas. Aircraft parking is based on H/D, NEW, and category of aircraft. Clearly identified by armament placards. Entries made in aircraft forms (entries made in 781a).

qas.q: What are general requirements for maintenance on explosive loaded aircraft?
qas.a: Munitions download for aircraft placed in fuel barn, confined space is required, hot work, repair location limits fire response.

qas.q: When is maintenance authorized inside hangars?
qas.a:
Munitions downloaded before hangaring
Exception #1 - single explosives loaded aircraft inside a hardened aircraft shelter (HAS) is not considered in a hangar for maintenance purposes
Exception #2 - Hail Storm

qas.q: What are general requirements for maintenance on explosive loaded aircraft?
qas.a:
Parked in designated area

Personnel accessing cockpit must meet AFI 21-101 guidelines

Maintenance won't increase probability of fire, stores release, weapons launch/arming, functioning of explosives or munitions, create any other unsafe environment

Armament systems must be electrically and mechanically safed before performing maintenance on the system requiring electrical power

Certain maintenance actions require download of chaff, photoflash, flare, and decoy cartridges before starting

qas.q: Forward firing munitions must not be stored how?
qas.a: Pointing towards the barracks or pointing towards support equipment

qas.q: What are hardened/protective aircraft shelter (HAS/PAS)/Flow through revetments (FTR)?
qas.a: Assures aircraft will remain operable if there is an explosion in an adjacent shelter or ready service storage facility

qas.q: What is Quantity distance (QD)?
qas.a: Refers to protection requirements from potential explosion site (PES) to different kinds of exposed sites (ES). Standards developed from explosive mishaps, tests, analyses. QD separations based on acceptable levels of damage between a PES and ES. QD separation not applicable in transport

qas.q: Quantity Distance is the relationship between what and what?
qas.a: Explosive quantity and distance separation (origins = 1965 Bien Hoa Disaaster). Provides defined types of protection. Distances are not absolute safe distances. Based on acceptable risk.

qas.q: What is a potential explosion site (PES)?
qas.a: Location or facility that contains or is expected to contain explosives. Examples: igloo in MSA. Bomb build up pad in MSA. HAM on flight line.

qas.q: How much earth covering (top, rear, and sides) must a earth covered igloo have?
qas.a: 24 inches. Rated for 500,000 pounds of explosives

qas.q: What is an exposed site (ES)?
qas.a: Any permanent structure or utility (may or may not be related to the explosive handling mission) at risk from either blast or fire effects of a PES. Examples: AMU building or hangar. May or may not contain explosives.

qas.q: What is the net explosive weight (NEW)?
qas.a: Total weight of all explosive, propellant and pyrotechnic in one item. Total weight of weapon vs total weight of explosives (percentage). Used for transportation

qas.q: What is the NEWQD used for?
qas.a: Used to figure explosives sitting. Normally equal to or less than NEW.

qas.q: What are the H/D 1.2 subdivisions?
qas.a: Effects produced by functioning of H/D 1.2 items vary with weight and size. Separated into three subdivisions in order to account for the differences in magnitude of these effects for QD Criteria. H/D 1.2.1 is the most hazardous and requires special consideration.

qas.q: What is the maximum credible event (MCE - 1.2.1 only)?
qas.a: Largest quantity of explosives expected to explode at one time when a stack is involved in a fire. When two stacks are too close together, they go off as one big stack. QD criteria allows for separate smaller explosions.

qas.q: What is the inhabited building distance (IBD)?
qas.a: Protects facilities and personnel not directly related to explosive operations

qas.q: What is the public traffic route distance (PTRD)?
qas.a: Required to protect public traffic routes

qas.q: What is the intraline distance (ILD)?
qas.a: Protect activities associated with explosives storage and operations

qas.q: What is the inter-magazine distance (IMD)?
qas.a: Between PESs to prevent one PES from simultaneously detonating an adjacent PES

qas.q: All ACC aircraft are considered what?
qas.a: All considered combat aircraft. Sited at Inter-magazine distance.

qas.q: What do externally loaded aircraft measure from?
qas.a: Munitions

qas.q: What do internally loaded aircraft measure from?
qas.a: Edge of the cargo hold

qas.q: What are barricades?
qas.a: Protect against low and high speed fragments

qas.q: What allows us to determine explosive content of items?
qas.a: Joint Hazard Classification System (JHCS).

qas.q: Who assumes the risk for shortening QD distances?
qas.a: Senior leadership IAW risk management principles
